name: Broadcast Caught-On-TV (Square)
description: Single-character 'caught on a live TV broadcast' trend recreation — audience-cam reaction beat from a real sports / news / award-show feed, anchored on the user's own selfie. Two-stage stack: gpt-5.4-image-2 produces a 1:1 SQUARE broadcast-capture still (1024x1024) → kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro i2v carries identity forward for 15s with native ambient stadium / venue audio. The critical, unintuitive rule: render the anchor SQUARE, not 9:16. Real broadcast cameras shoot 16:9, so strict portrait reads as AI-generated; square 1:1 'splits the difference' and feels like a phone capture of a TV screen. Pronoun-swap the trend prompt to match the user's identity before submit. The format is one-clip, not a multi-scene cut — Kling delivers the entire 15s read with diegetic audio.
referenceNotes: User selfie / character reference IS required — the entire format is 'this is me, caught on TV'. Pass it once into the image stage via `--ref <selfie>`; the resulting still does identity work for Kling, so no second ref on the i2v stage. AI-improvised faces break the format (the joke depends on the viewer recognizing the on-screen person as the poster). If the brief names a specific real broadcast (KBO, NFL, Wimbledon, Eurovision, etc.) reproduce its on-screen graphics conventions verbatim — never invent fictional broadcast brands, they read fake.

constants.structure: 0-3s subject watches event / unaware → 3-6s notices camera → 6-12s amused reaction beat (sip / laugh / clap) → 12-15s settles back, ambient continues
constants.aspectRatioRule: Render the anchor still SQUARE (1024x1024). Real broadcast cams shoot 16:9 — strict 9:16 portrait feels AI-generated. Square 'splits the difference' and reads as a phone capture of a live TV screen. Crop / pad to platform-strict 9:16 only at final delivery, never at origin.
constants.captionStyle: none on the raw clip — ambient + on-screen broadcast graphics carry. Optional Hormozi-style caption layer added at composition time if posted with a hot-take overlay.
constants.musicDefault: off — Kling produces diegetic stadium / venue ambience natively. Explicitly negate music + studio voiceover in the prompt or Kling will add a soundtrack.

variationAxes: subject angle (3/4 medium, frontal close-up, profile cutaway)
graphics density (score-bug only / score+lower-third / full broadcast package with ticker)

scene01StillSquareExample.note: Example 1024x1024 broadcast-capture still from the source project (kbo-broadcast-001, gpt-5.4-image-2). Reference of the look — square framing, SPOTV-style soft compression, stadium seating context.

scene01VideoExample.note: Example 15s 960x960 square Kling i2v output from kbo-broadcast-001 — the user-preferred winner. Anchored on the gpt-5.4-image-2 still above; demonstrates how Kling honors the square first-frame over the --aspect-ratio flag.

slots.description: Sports league / live broadcast context. Examples: KBO baseball, NFL, NBA, Wimbledon, Premier League, Eurovision, awards-show audience cutaway, news B-roll.
What kind of caught-on-TV moment. Examples: audience-cam reaction, kiss-cam, jumbotron-cam, press-pool flash, red-carpet cutaway, news vox-pop.
On-screen graphics package style. Examples: SPOTV, ESPN, Sky Sports, BBC, TV Tokyo. Drives the lower-third + score-bug aesthetic.
Language Kling synthesizes the ambient announcer in. Examples: Korean (KBO), English (US-network), Japanese, Portuguese.
Stadium / arena / venue identifier. Examples: SK Wyverns home, Centre Court, Madison Square Garden, Berlin awards stage.
How the user-as-spectator appears. Examples: seated spectator, standing in crowd, press row, front-row VIP.
Distinctive event-specific sound (bat-crack, crowd-roar, applause-swell, photo-shutter burst).
Ambient venue audio bed (stadium hum, arena PA distortion, press-pool murmur).
Pronouns for the subject. Default they/them; override per use case.

doNotCopyLiterally: 1:1 SQUARE aspect — this is the entire trick. 9:16 portrait kills the illusion.. gpt-5.4-image-2 to kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro stack — both load-bearing. Light broadcast compression noise + flyaway hairs + slight natural sweat sheen — keeps the still from looking studio-AI. Slight broadcast camera shake on Kling i2v — locked-tripod kills it. Ambient announcer murmur synthesized by Kling --audio, not ElevenLabs VO
stackSummary: gpt-5.4-image-2 (1024² square anchor) then kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro 15s i2v with --audio for ambient announcer + crowd. Music banned in the Kling prompt. No editor stage — single clip out.
